wiki: This knowledge base contains general encyclopedic facts from Wikipedia. It covers a wide range of topics including people, places, organizations, historical events, media works (films, TV shows, music), sports, and geography. Each entry typically describes a single named entity with concise factual information. It is suitable for general-knowledge questions about who, what, when, where.
bioasq: This knowledge base contains biomedical literature snippets from PubMed articles. It covers medical topics including diseases, genes, proteins, drugs, treatments, clinical trials, and biological mechanisms. Each entry is an excerpt from a peer-reviewed biomedical research paper. It is suitable for biomedical factoid questions about genes, proteins, diseases, drugs, diagnostic methods, and medical procedures.
sciq: This knowledge base contains science educational content covering physics, chemistry, biology, earth science, and life sciences. Each entry is an explanatory paragraph from science textbooks or educational materials, describing scientific concepts, processes, and phenomena. It is suitable for answering science exam questions about natural phenomena, chemical reactions, biological processes, physical laws, and earth science topics.
